title: Same-shard completion can clear an unrelated active rebuild job and inflate rebuild-completed counters
evidence: recordVectorShardRebuildCompleted now clears activeJobId when degradedVectorHealth.lastShard === completedShard, even if degradedVectorHealth.activeJobId !== input.jobId. reindex.ts also calls completeVectorShardRecovery from the non-repair path when degradedVectorMatchesShard(activeShardPath), so a normal reindex can call recordVectorShardRebuildCompleted without being the active repair job.
rationale: Before this commit, rebuild completion cleared activeJobId only for the matching job id, preserving the meaning of activeJobId and rebuildsCompleted as repair-job lifecycle metrics. After this change, any completion for the same shard, including a non-repair reindex or a duplicate same-shard repair, can mark degradedVectorHealth healthy, null activeJobId, and increment rebuildsCompleted while another repair job may still be active. That is a bounded observability regression for dashboards/tests depending on job identity and counter semantics; retrieval scoring itself is not changed.
